pi_items.metadata_value: CRA T5 Slip Format 2024 and CRA Form T1206 (Tax on Split Income)
pi_items.verified_value: CRA T5 Slip Format 2024 and CRA Form T1206 (Tax on Split Income)
pi_items.source: Task description and verifier logic confirm the use of T5 and T1206 tax forms, which are real CRA forms. However, the data entered into them is synthetic.
Task description and verifier.py both explicitly require the name Chloe Tremblay.
The province 'ON' is not mentioned in the task description. Because the setup and data files are completely missing from the environment, there is no way to confirm the province.
Task description and verifier.py state the expected output file is 'chloe_tremblay.24t' in the 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\StudioTax\' directory.

pi_items.metadata_value: Tax on Split Income (TOSI) requires explicitly flagging the T5 dividend so it flows to Form T1206 and is taxed at the highest marginal rate. Also tests full-time student scholarship exemption.
pi_items.verified_value: Tax on Split Income (TOSI) requires explicitly flagging the T5 dividend so it flows to Form T1206 and is taxed at the highest marginal rate. Also tests full-time student scholarship exemption.
pi_items.source: Verifier.py heavily penalizes the agent if the TOSI flag is not successfully applied to the $25,000 T5 dividend, capping the score at 60.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on a synthetic tax scenario for a taxpayer named Chloe Tremblay. It requires explicitly flagging a $25,000 T5 dividend for Tax on Split Income (TOSI) / Form T1206. The expected output file path is 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\StudioTax\chloe_tremblay.24t'. However, I discovered a critical defect: 'setup_task.ps1', 'export_result.ps1', and the data file 'scenario_tremblay_tosi.txt' are entirely missing from the environment. This breaks the scenario generation and makes the task programmatically unscorable because 'tosi_result.json' is never generated.
